quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Population viability analysis (PVA)"?

options.text: Cyclins accumulate and activate CDKs at specific cell cycle stages, driving progression through phosphorylation of target proteins
ATP production driven by the proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane; H⁺ flows through ATP synthase from lumen to stroma
A mathematical model estimating a population's probability of persistence over a specified time period, incorporating demographic and environmental variability
A 10-step metabolic pathway in the cytoplasm that converts glucose (6C) into 2 pyruvate (3C), producing 2 net ATP and 2 NADH

quiz_questions.explanation: "Population viability analysis (PVA)" is defined as: A mathematical model estimating a population's probability of persistence over a specified time period, incorporating demographic and environmental variability. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Minimum viable population (MVP)"?

options.text: Each diploid organism has two alleles for each gene; these separate during meiosis so each gamete carries only one allele
Normal cells stop dividing when they contact neighboring cells; cancer cells lose this control (contact inhibition)
The smallest population size that can sustain itself over a long period (typically 50/500 rule: 50 for short-term inbreeding avoidance, 500 for long-term genetic diversity)
The pathway by which a chemical element (C, N, P, H₂O) moves through the biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem

quiz_questions.explanation: "Minimum viable population (MVP)" is defined as: The smallest population size that can sustain itself over a long period (typically 50/500 rule: 50 for short-term inbreeding avoidance, 500 for long-term genetic diversity). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Effective population size (Ne)"?

options.text: The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success
RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures
S = cAᶻ; the number of species increases with habitat area, where z ≈ 0.3 for islands and c is a constant
The energy available to do work; ΔG  0 (endergonic, requires energy input), ΔG = 0 (equilibrium)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Effective population size (Ne)" is defined as: The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Wildlife corridor"?

options.text: A shared derived character that defines a clade; evidence of common ancestry (e.g., feathers in birds)
The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites
A three-nucleotide sequence in mRNA that codes for a specific amino acid or stop signal; 64 total codons, 61 code for amino acids
A connected strip of habitat linking isolated patches, enabling gene flow, migration, and recolonization between fragmented populations

quiz_questions.explanation: "Wildlife corridor" is defined as: A connected strip of habitat linking isolated patches, enabling gene flow, migration, and recolonization between fragmented populations. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Ecosystem services"?

options.text: Insertion or deletion of nucleotides (not in multiples of 3) that shifts the reading frame, altering all downstream amino acids
Benefits humans receive from ecosystems: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, flood control), cultural (recreation), and supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation)
The 4-carbon molecule that accepts acetyl-CoA to begin each turn of the Krebs cycle; regenerated at the end of each turn
Barriers preventing gene flow: prezygotic (habitat, temporal, behavioral, mechanical, gametic) and postzygotic (hybrid inviability, sterility)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Ecosystem services" is defined as: Benefits humans receive from ecosystems: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, flood control), cultural (recreation), and supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A mathematical model estimating a population's probability of persistence over a specified time period, incorporating demographic and environmental variability"?

options.text: Biodiversity hotspot
Cohesion
Ribosome
Population viability analysis (PVA)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Population viability analysis (PVA)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The smallest population size that can sustain itself over a long period (typically 50/500 rule: 50 for short-term inbreeding avoidance, 500 for long-term genetic diversity)"?

options.text: Ribosome
Minimum viable population (MVP)
Codominance
Photosystem II (P680)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Minimum viable population (MVP)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success"?

options.text: r-selected species
Effective population size (Ne)
Michaelis-Menten kinetics
Crossing over

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Effective population size (Ne)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A connected strip of habitat linking isolated patches, enabling gene flow, migration, and recolonization between fragmented populations"?

options.text: Angiogenesis
Wildlife corridor
Biodiversity hotspot
Phospholipid

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Wildlife corridor".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Benefits humans receive from ecosystems: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, flood control), cultural (recreation), and supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation)"?

options.text: Oxidative phosphorylation
Missense mutation
Leading strand
Ecosystem services

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Ecosystem services".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Conservation Biology is accurate?

options.text: The enzyme RuBisCO catalyzes carbon fixation (CO₂ + RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA), making it the most abundant protein on Earth.
Conservation Biology integrates ecology, genetics, and evolutionary biology to protect and restore biological diversity.
Exponential growth (dN/dt = rN) occurs when resources are unlimited, producing J-shaped curves.
Enzyme specificity arises from the precise 3D shape of the active site, which is determined by the amino acid sequence.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Conservation Biology as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Conservation Biology?

options.text: ATP hydrolysis (ΔG = -30.5 kJ/mol) couples to endergonic reactions to drive cellular work.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
Population viability analysis (PVA) estimates extinction probability for small populations considering demographic and environmental stochasticity.
Sexual selection and artificial selection are special cases of selection that shape traits beyond survival.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Conservation Biology as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Conservation Biology?

options.text: Parsimony (fewest evolutionary changes) and maximum likelihood methods are used to determine the most probable tree topology.
Minimum viable population (MVP), effective population size (Ne), and genetic rescue inform conservation management.
Populations are characterized by density, age structure, survivorship curves (Type I, II, III), and life history strategies (r-selected vs.
Genetic mapping constructs a linear order of genes along chromosomes based on recombination frequencies.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Conservation Biology as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Conservation Biology?

options.text: Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.
Helicase unwinds the double helix, primase synthesizes RNA primers, and DNA polymerase III synthesizes new strands in the 5'→3' direction.
DNA Replication and Transcription describes how genetic information stored in DNA is copied (replication) and read into mRNA (transcription).
Landscape ecology connects habitat patches through corridors, and ecosystem services valuation provides economic arguments for conservation.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Conservation Biology as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Conservation Biology?

options.text: Genomic technologies (DNA sequencing, gene editing with CRISPR-Cas9, and bioinformatics) have revolutionized biology, medicine, agriculture, and forensics.
The central dogma of molecular biology—DNA → RNA → Protein—describes the flow of genetic information in cells.
Conservation Biology integrates ecology, genetics, and evolutionary biology to protect and restore biological diversity.
Monohybrid crosses (Aa × Aa → 3:1 phenotypic ratio) and dihybrid crosses (AaBb × AaBb → 9:3:3:1 ratio) predict offspring ratios, testable with chi-square analysis.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Conservation Biology as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Conservation Biology?

options.text: Cancer epigenetics: abnormal DNA methylation patterns silencing tumor suppressor genes or activating oncogenes without DNA mutations
Sickle cell trait (HbAS heterozygotes) conferring malaria resistance while homozygotes (HbSS) suffer sickle cell disease—a classic example of heterozygote advantage
Metabolic syndrome and diabetes: dysregulation of glucose metabolism affecting cellular respiration, insulin signaling, and energy storage pathways
Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Conservation Biology.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Conservation Biology?

options.text: Genetic rescue of the Florida panther: introducing 8 female Texas pumas in 1995 restored genetic diversity and tripled the population from ~25 to ~200 individuals
Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes
Endosymbiotic theory evidence: mitochondria and chloroplasts have double membranes, own circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, and replicate independently
Mass extinctions (e.g., K-Pg, 66 MYA) eliminating 75% of species but opening ecological niches that enabled mammalian diversification and eventual human evolution

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Conservation Biology.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Conservation Biology applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Citrate as a food additive and preservative, derived from the first intermediate of the Krebs cycle, produced industrially by Aspergillus niger fermentation
ABO blood type compatibility in transfusions determined by codominant Iᴬ and Iᴮ alleles and recessive i allele (3 alleles, 6 genotypes, 4 phenotypes)
Detecting natural selection by comparing observed genotype frequencies to Hardy-Weinberg predictions and identifying significant deviations using chi-square tests
Ecosystem services valuation estimating that pollination by insects is worth $235-577 billion annually globally, providing economic justification for pollinator conservation

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Conservation Biology.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Conservation Biology?

options.text: Targeted cancer therapy: Herceptin (trastuzumab) blocking the HER2 receptor in breast cancers that overexpress this oncogene protein
Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production
Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration
DNA profiling (forensic fingerprinting) using short tandem repeat (STR) polymorphisms for criminal identification with error rates below 1 in a billion

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Conservation Biology.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Conservation Biology in practice?

options.text: Antioxidant research examining whether dietary antioxidants (vitamins C, E) can reduce oxidative DNA damage from reactive oxygen species
Prion diseases (mad cow, CJD) where misfolded proteins induce normal proteins to refold incorrectly, demonstrating the critical importance of protein conformation
Genetic rescue of the Florida panther: introducing 8 female Texas pumas in 1995 restored genetic diversity and tripled the population from ~25 to ~200 individuals
Targeted cancer therapy: Herceptin (trastuzumab) blocking the HER2 receptor in breast cancers that overexpress this oncogene protein

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Conservation Biology.
